translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided them, as We had guided Noah before them, and of his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus We reward those who are upright and do good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob); We guided all of them; and We guided Nooh before them and of his descendants, Dawud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Moosa and Haroon; and this is the way We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave to him Isaac and Jacob -- each one We guided, And Noah We guided before; and of his seed David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron -- even so We recompense the good-doers --

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Is-haq and Ya'qub: each one We guided. And Nuh We had guided afore, and of his progeny Da-ud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Musa and Harun. And thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh (Noah), and among his progeny Dawud (David), Sulaiman (Solomon), Ayub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses), and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob—each of them We guided. And We guided Noah previously; and from his descendants David, and Solomon, and Job, and Joseph, and Moses, and Aaron. Thus We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon Abraham (offspring) Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob) and each of them did We guide to the right way as We had earlier guided Noah to the right way; and (of his descendants We guided) Da'ud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon), Ayyub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq and Ya`qub, each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh and among his progeny Dawud, Sulayman, Ayyub, Yusuf, Musa, and Harun. Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob; each of them We guided; and Noah did We guide aforetime; and of his seed (We guided) David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided both; and We guided Noah before them, among his descendants were David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron as such, We recompense the gooddoers,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - all [of them] We guided. And Noah, We guided before; and among his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob. Both had received Our guidance. Noah received Our guidance before Abraham and so did his descendants: David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron. Thus is the reward for the righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub; each did We guide, and Nuh did We guide before, and of his descendants, Dawood and Sulaiman and Ayub and Yusuf and Musa and Haroun; and thus do We reward those who do good (to others).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob, each of whom We guided as We had guided Noah before. Among his descendants were David and Solomon, and Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob: all (three) guided: and before him, We guided Noah, and among his progeny, David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron: thus do We reward those who do good:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We bestowed Solomon on David who was an excellent devotee, for he turned to God in penitence.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed Sulaiman to Dawud; what an excellent bondman! He is indeed most inclined.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave unto David Solomon; how excellent a servant he was! He was a penitent.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We vouchsafed Unto Da-ud Sulaiman. How excellent a bondman! j Verily he was oft-returning.'

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And to Dawud (David) We gave Sulaiman (Solomon). How excellent (a) slave! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We granted David, Solomon, an excellent servant. He was penitent.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We bestowed upon David (an illustrious son), Solomon. How excellent a servant (of Ours he was)! Indeed he constantly turned to Us in devotion.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And to Dawud We gave Sulayman. How excellent a servant! Verily, he was ever turning in repentance (to Us)!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed on David, Solomon. How excellent a slave! Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (toward Allah).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave Solomon to David; and he was an excellent worshiper, he was penitent.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And to David We gave Solomon. An excellent servant, indeed he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We granted Solomon to David, a blessed servant of Ours and certainly the most repentant person.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave to Dawood Sulaiman, most excellent the servant! Surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave David Solomon. He was an excellent servant who always turned to God.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: To David We gave Solomon (for a son),- How excellent in Our service! Ever did he turn (to Us)!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We have sent revelations to you as We sent revelations to Noah and the prophets (who came) after him; and We sent revelations to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob, and their offspring, and to Jesus and Job, and to Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and to David We gave the Book of Psalms,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed We sent a divine revelation to you (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessing be upon him) as We did send divine revelations to Nooh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; and We sent divine revelations to Ibrahim (Abraham) and Ismael (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob) and their offspring, and Eisa (Jesus) and Ayyub (Job) and Yunus (Jonah) and Haroon (Aaron) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and We bestowed the Zaboor (the Holy Book) upon Dawud (David).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We have revealed to thee as We revealed to Noah, and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We have revealed unto thee even as We revealed unto Nuh and the prophets after him, and as We revealed unto Ibrahim and Isma'il and Is-haq and Ya'qub and the tribes, and 'Isa and Ayyub and Yunus and Harun and Sulaiman, and unto Daud We vouchsafed a Scripture.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, We have inspired you (O Muhammad SAW) as We inspired Nuh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; We (also) inspired Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), Ya'qub (Jacob), and Al-Asbat [the twelve sons of Ya'qub (Jacob)], 'Iesa (Jesus), Ayub (Job), Yunus (Jonah), Harun (Aaron), and Sulaiman (Solomon), and to Dawud (David) We gave the Zabur (Psalms).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We have inspired you, as We had inspired Noah and the prophets after him. And We inspired Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the Patriarchs, and Jesus, and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron, and Solomon. And We gave David the Psalms.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Muhammad!) We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the offspring of Jacob, and Jesus and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, We have sent the revelation to you as We sent the revelation to Nuh and the Prophets after him; We (also) sent the revelation to Ibrahim, Isma`il, Ishaq, Ya`qub, and Al-Asbat, (the offspring of the twelve sons of Ya`qub) `Isa, Ayyub, Yunus, Harun, and Sulayman; and to Dawud We gave the Zabur.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! We inspire thee as We inspired Noah and the prophets after him, as We inspired Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the tribes, and Jesus and Job and Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and as We imparted unto David the Psalms;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and to the Prophets after him, and We revealed to (Prophets) Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave to David the Psalms.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him. And we revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the Descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and to David We gave the book [of Psalms].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), We have sent revelations to you just as were sent to Noah and the Prophets who lived after him and to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, his descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. We gave the Psalms to David.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We have revealed to you as We revealed to Nuh, and the prophets after him, and We revealed to Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq and Yaqoub and the tribes, and Isa and Ayub and Yunus and Haroun and Sulaiman and We gave to Dawood

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have sent revelation to you [Prophet] as We did to Noah and the prophets who came after him, to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon and David, to whom We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We have sent thee inspiration, as We sent it to Noah and the Messengers after him: we sent inspiration to Abraham, Isma'il, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and solomon, and to David We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Remember) David and Solomon, when they pronounced judgement about the field which was eaten up at night by sheep belonging to certain people. We were witness to their judgement.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Dawud and Sulaiman, when they were deciding the dispute of a field, when some people’s sheep had strayed into it at night; and We were Present at the time of their deciding.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And David and Solomon -- when they gave judgment concerning the tillage, when the sheep of the people strayed there, and We bore witness to their judgment;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Daud and Sulaiman! recall what time they gave judgment regarding the tillage when certain people's sheep had pastured therein at night; and of the judgment concerning them We were the Witnesses.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Dawud (David) and Sulaiman (Solomon), when they gave judgement in the case of the field in which the sheep of certain people had pastured at night and We were witness to their judgement.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And David and Solomon, when they gave judgment in the case of the field, when some people’s sheep wandered therein by night; and We were witnesses to their judgment.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We bestowed the same favour upon David and Solomon. Recall, when they gave judgement regarding a tillage into which the sheep of some people had strayed at night, and We were witnesses to their judgement.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Dawud and Sulayman, when they gave judgement in the case of the field in which the sheep of certain people had Nafashat; and We were witness to their judgement.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And David and Solomon, when they gave judgment concerning the field, when people's sheep had strayed and browsed therein by night; and We were witnesses to their judgment.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And David and Solomon they passed judgment upon the tilled land on which the people? s sheep had strayed. We bore witness to their judgment, and

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] David and Solomon, when they judged concerning the field - when the sheep of a people overran it [at night], and We were witness to their judgement.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: David and Solomon were trying to settle the case of the people's sheep that grazed in a corn-field at night. We witnessed their decree in that matter.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Dawood and Sulaiman when they gave judgment concerning the field when the people's sheep pastured therein by night, and We were bearers of witness to their judgment.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Tell of David and Solomon who both passed judgement on the field into which some people's sheep had strayed [and grazed] at night. We bore witness to their judgement.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember David and Solomon, when they gave judgment in the matter of the field into which the sheep of certain people had strayed by night: We did witness their judgment.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We made Solomon understand the case, and bestowed on each wisdom and knowledge, We subdued the al-jibal (mountains) with David to sing Our praises, and at-tair (birds). It is We who did it.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We explained the case to Sulaiman; and to both We gave the kingdom and knowledge; and subjected the hills to proclaim the Purity along with Dawud, and (also subjected) the birds; and these were Our works.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We made Solomon to understand it, and unto each gave We judgment and knowledge. And with David We subjected the mountains to give glory, and the birds, and We were doers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So We gave the understanding thereof unto Sulaiman; and unto each We vouchsafed judgment and knowledge. And We so subjected the mountains that they shall hallow us along with Daud, and also the birds; and We were the doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We made Sulaiman (Solomon) to understand (the case), and to each of them We gave Hukman (right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify Our Praises along with Dawud (David), And it was We Who were the doers (of all these things).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And so We made Solomon understand it, and to each We gave wisdom and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains along with David to sing Our praises, and the birds as well—surely We did.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We guided Solomon to the right verdict, and We had granted each of them judgement and knowledge. We made the mountains and the birds celebrate the praise of Allah with David. It was We Who did all this.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We made Sulayman to understand (the case); and to each of them We gave wisdom (Hukm) and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify Our praises along with Dawud. And it was We Who were the doer (of all these things).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We made Solomon to understand (the case); and unto each of them We gave judgment and knowledge. And we subdued the hills and the birds to hymn (His) praise along with David. We were the doers (thereof).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We made Solomon to understand it, and to both We gave judgment and knowledge. And with David We subjected the mountains and birds to exalt (Allah). All this We have done.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave understanding of the case to Solomon, and to each [of them] We gave judgement and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains to exalt [Us], along with David and [also] the birds. And We were doing [that].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made Solomon understand the law about the case and gave both David and Solomon knowledge and wisdom. We made the mountains and birds glorify the Lord along with David. We had also done to him such favors before.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We made Sulaiman to understand it; and to each one We gave wisdom and knowledge; and We made the mountains, and the birds to celebrate Our praise with Dawood; and We were the doers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave Solomon the right understanding of the matter, and We bestowed wisdom and knowledge on both of them. We caused the mountains and the birds to celebrate Our praises along with David. We had the power to do this --

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: To Solomon We inspired the (right) understanding of the matter: to each (of them) We gave Judgment and Knowledge; it was Our power that made the hills and the birds celebrate Our praises, with David: it was We Who did (all these things).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We gave David and Solomon knowledge; and they said: "All praise be to God who has favoured us over many of His creatures who believe."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We indeed bestowed great knowledge to Dawud and Sulaiman; and they both said, “All praise is to Allah, Who bestowed us superiority over many of His believing bondmen.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave David and Solomon knowledge and they said, 'Praise belongs to God who has preferred us over many of His believing servants.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed unto Daud and Sulaiman a knowledge, and the twain said: praise unto Allah Who hath preferred us above many of His believing bondmen!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed We gave knowledge to Dawud (David) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and they both said: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has preferred us above many of His believing slaves!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave David and Solomon knowledge. They said, “Praise God, who has favored us over many of His believing servants.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (On the other hand), We granted knowledge to David and Solomon and they said: "All praise be to Allah Who has exalted us above many of His believing servants!"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed We gave knowledge to Dawud and Sulayman, and they both said: "All praise be to Allah, Who has preferred us above many of His believing servants!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We verily gave knowledge unto David and Solomon, and they said: Praise be to Allah, Who hath preferred us above many of His believing slaves!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave knowledge to David and Solomon. They said: 'Praise be to Allah who has preferred us above many of His believing worshipers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, "Praise [is due] to Allah, who has favored us over many of His believing servants."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave knowledge to David and Soloman. They said, "It is only God who deserves all praise. He has exalted us above many of His believing servants."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We gave knowledge to Dawood and Sulaiman, and they both said: Praise be to Allah, Who has made us to excel many of His believing servants.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We bestowed knowledge on David and Solomon and they both said, "Praise be to God who has exalted us above many of His believing servants."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We gave (in the past) knowledge to David and Solomon: And they both said: "Praise be to Allah, Who has favoured us above many of his servants who believe!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Solomon was heir to David, and he said: "O people, we have been taught the language of Tair, and have been given of everything. This is a clear favour indeed."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And Sulaiman became Dawud’s heir; and he said, “O people, we have indeed been taught the language of birds, and have been given from all things; this surely is a manifest favour.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Solomon was David's heir, and he said, 'Men, we have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given of everything; surely this is indeed the manifest bounty.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Sulaiman inherited from Daud. And he said: O mankind! verily have been taught the diction of we birds, and we have been vcuchsafed of every thing; and verily this is grace manifest.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Sulaiman (Solomon) inherited (the knowledge of) Dawud (David). He said: "O mankind! We have been taught the language of birds, and on us have been bestowed all things. This, verily, is an evident grace (from Allah)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Solomon succeeded David. He said, “O people, we were taught the language of birds, and we were given from everything. This is indeed a real blessing.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Solomon succeeded David and said: "O people, we have been taught the speech of birds and we have been endowed with all kinds of things. Surely this is a conspicuous favour (from Allah)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Sulayman inherited from Dawud. He said: "O mankind! We have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from everything. This, verily, is an evident grace."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Solomon was David's heir. And he said: O mankind! Lo! we have been taught the language of birds, and have been given (abundance) of all things. This surely is evident favour.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Solomon inherited David. He said: 'Know, my people, we have been taught the speech of birds and given everything. Surely, this is a clear bounty'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Solomon became the heir to David. He said, "People, we have been taught the language of the birds and have been granted a share of everything. This indeed is a manifest favor (from God)".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Sulaiman was Dawood's heir, and he said: O men! we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given all things; most surely this is manifest grace.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Solomon succeeded David. He said, "Know, my people, that we have been taught the speech of birds and endowed with all good things. Surely this is God's manifest grace."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Solomon was David's heir. He said: "O ye people! We have been taught the speech of birds, and on us has been bestowed (a little) of all things: this is indeed Grace manifest (from Allah.)"

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Apostle said, "There were two women with whom there were their two sons. A wolf came and took away the son of one of them. That lady said to her companion, 'The wolf has taken your son.' The other said, 'But it has taken your son.' So both of them sought the judgment of (the Prophet) David who judged that the boy should be given to the older lady. Then both of them went to (the Prophet) Solomon, son of David and informed him of the case. Solomon said, 'Give me a knife so that I may cut the child into two portions and give one half to each of you.' The younger lady said, 'Do not do so; may Allah bless you ! He is her child.' On that, he gave the child to the younger lady." Abu Huraira added: By Allah! I had never heard the word 'Sakkin' as meaning knife, except on that day, for we used to call it 'Mudya"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We (subjugated) the wind to Solomon. Its morning's journey took one month, and the evening's one month. We made a spring of molten brass to flow for him; and many jinns laboured for him by the will of his Lord. Anyone of them who turned from Our command was made to taste the torment of blazing fire.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We gave the wind in Sulaiman’s control – its morning journey equal to a month’s course and the evening journey equal to a month’s course; and We sprung a stream of molten copper for him; and from the jinns, who worked before him by the command of his Lord; and those among them who turned away from Our command – We shall make them taste the punishment of the blazing fire.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And to Solomon the wind; its morning course was a month's journey, and its evening course was a month's journey. And We made the Fount of Molten Brass to flow for him. And of the jinn, some worked before him by the leave of his Lord; and such of them as swerved away from Our commandment, We would let them taste the chastisement of the Blaze;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Unto Sulaiman We subjected the wind, whereof the morning journeying was a month and the evening journeying a month. And We made a fount of brass to flow for him. And of the Jinn were some who worked before him by the Will Of his Lord. And whosoever of them swerved from Our command, him We shall cause to taste the torment of the Blaze.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And to Solomon (We subjected) the wind, its morning (stride from sunrise till midnoon) was a month's (journey), and its afternoon (stride from the midday decline of the sun to sunset) was a month's (journey i.e. in one day he could travel two months' journey). And We caused a fount of (molten) brass to flow for him, and there were jinns that worked in front of him, by the Leave of his Lord, and whosoever of them turned aside from Our Command, We shall cause him to taste of the torment of the blazing Fire.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And for Solomon the wind—its outward journey was one month, and its return journey was one month. And We made a spring of tar flow for him. And there were sprites that worked under him, by the leave of his Lord. But whoever of them swerved from Our command, We make him taste of the punishment of the Inferno.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We subdued the wind to Solomon: its morning course was a month's journey and its evening course was a month's journey. We gave him a spring flowing with molten brass, and We subdued for him jinn who, by his Lord's permission, worked before him. Such of them as swerved from Our commandment, We let them taste the chastisement of the Blazing Fire.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And to Sulayman (We subjected) the wind, its morning was a month's (journey), and its afternoon was a month's (journey). And We caused a fount of Qitr to flow for him, and there were Jinn that worked in front of him, by the leave of his Lord. And whosoever of them turned aside from Our command, We shall cause him to taste of the torment of the blazing Fire.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And unto Solomon (We gave) the wind, whereof the morning course was a month's journey and the evening course a month's journey, and We caused the fount of copper to gush forth for him, and (We gave him) certain of the jinn who worked before him by permission of his Lord. And such of them as deviated from Our command, them We caused to taste the punishment of flaming Fire.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: To Solomon the morning course of the wind was a month's journey, and its evening course was also a month's journey. We caused copper to be (as a) molten spring for him. And the jinn, some served him by the permission of his Lord. But as for those amongst them that swerved away from Our Command, We shall let them taste the punishment of the Blaze (the Fire).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind - its morning [journey was that of] a month - and its afternoon [journey was that of] a month, and We made flow for him a spring of [liquid] copper. And among the jinn were those who worked for him by the permission of his Lord. And whoever deviated among them from Our command - We will make him taste of the punishment of the Blaze.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (We made subservient to) Solomon the wind that travelled a month's journey in the morning and a month's journey in the evening. We made a stream of brass flow for him and some of the jinn worked for him by his Lord's command. We would make whichever of them (jinn) who turned away from Our command to suffer a burning torment.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And (We made) the wind (subservient) to Sulaiman, which made a month's journey in the morning and a month's journey in the evening, and We made a fountain of molten copper to flow out for him, and of the jinn there were those who worked before him by the command of his Lord; and whoever turned aside from Our command from among them, We made him taste of the punishment of burning.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We subjected the wind to Solomon; its morning course was a month and its evening course a month; and We caused a fount of molten copper to flow for him. And of the jinn there were some who worked under him, by the command of his Lord. If any one of them turned away from Our command, We would make him taste the punishment of the burning fire.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And to Solomon (We made) the Wind (obedient): Its early morning (stride) was a month's (journey), and its evening (stride) was a month's (journey); and We made a Font of molten brass to flow for him; and there were Jinns that worked in front of him, by the leave of his Lord, and if any of them turned aside from our command, We made him taste of the Penalty of the Blazing Fire.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So we subjugated the wind to his service which carried his merchandise wheresoever he wished;

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We therefore gave the wind under his control, moving steadily by his command wherever he wished.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So We subjected to him the wind, that ran at his commandment, softly, wherever he might light on,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We subjected to him the wind: it ran gently by his command withersoever he directed.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So, We subjected to him the wind, it blew gently to his order whithersoever he willed,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We placed the wind at his service, blowing gently by his command, wherever he directed.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We subjected the wind to him, so that it blew gently at his bidding, wherever he directed it,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, We subjected to him the wind; it blew gently by his order wherever he willed,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So We made the wind subservient unto him, setting fair by his command whithersoever he intended.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So We subjected the wind to him, so that it ran softly by his command wherever he wished;

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We subjected to him the wind blowing by his command, gently, wherever he directed,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made the wind subservient to him, to blow gently wherever he desired at his command

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then We made the wind subservient to him; it made his command to run gently wherever he desired,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then We subjected the wind to his power, so that it blew gently, at his behest, wherever he willed,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then We subjected the wind to his power, to flow gently to his order, Whithersoever he willed,-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And the devils -- the builders and divers of all kinds,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And made the demons subservient to him, all builders and divers.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and the Satans, every builder and diver

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We subjected to him the evil ones: every builder and diver.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And also the Shayatin (devils) from the jinns (including) every kind of builder and diver,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And the demons—every builder and diver.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We also subjected the devils to him -- all kinds of builders and divers;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And the Shayatin, from every kind of builder and diver,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the unruly, every builder and diver (made We subservient),

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and the satans, every builder and diver

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [also] the devils [of jinn] - every builder and diver

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and all the devils who built and dived for him.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the shaitans, every builder and diver,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and also the jinn, every kind of builder and diver

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: As also the evil ones, (including) every kind of builder and diver,-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We made tempestuous winds obedient to Solomon which blew swiftly to sail at his bidding (with his ships) to the land We had blessed. We are cognisant of everything.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We subjected the fast wind for Sulaiman, which moved by his command towards the land in which We have placed blessings; and We know everything.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And to Solomon the wind, strongly blowing, that ran at his command unto the land that We had blessed; and We had knowledge of everything;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And unto Sulaiman We subjected the wind, strongly raging, running at his command toward the land wherein We had placed Our blessing; and of evrything We are the knowers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And to Sulaiman (Solomon) (We subjected) the wind strongly raging, running by his command towards the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are the All-Knower.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And to Solomon the stormy wind, blowing at His command towards the land that We have blessed. We are aware of everything.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We subdued the strongly raging wind to Solomon which blew at his bidding towards the land We blessed. We know everything.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And to Sulayman (We subjected) the wind strongly raging, running by his command towards the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are the All-Knower.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And unto Solomon (We subdued) the wind in its raging. It set by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are Aware.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: To Solomon the raging wind ran at his command to the land which We had blessed. We have knowledge of all things.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made subservient to Solomon the swift wind that blew on his command to the land in which We had sent blessings. We have the knowledge of all things.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And (We made subservient) to Sulaiman the wind blowing violent, pursuing its course by his command to the land which We had blessed, and We are knower of all things.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We subjected to Solomon the stormy wind, which blew at his behest towards the land which We had blessed. For it is We who have knowledge of all things --

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (It was Our power that made) the violent (unruly) wind flow (tamely) for Solomon, to his order, to the land which We had blessed: for We do know all things.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And many of the devils (We also made obedient to him) who dived for him, and did many other things; and We kept watch over them.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And among the devils, were those who dived (in water) for him and did works other than this; and We had kept them restrained.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and of the Satans some dived for him and did other work besides; and We were watching over them.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And of the satans were some who dove for him, and worked a work beside that; and of them We were the Watchers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And of the Shayatin (devils) (from the jinns) were some who dived for him, and did other work besides that; and it was We Who guarded them.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And of the devils were some that dived for him, and performed other, lesser tasks. But We kept them restrained.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We subdued many devils who dived (into the sea) for him and carried out other jobs besides that. We kept watch over all of them.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And of the Shayatin were some who dived for him, and did other work besides that; and it was We Who guarded them.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And of the evil ones (subdued We unto him) some who dived (for pearls) for him and did other work, and We were warders unto them.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And some of the satans dived for him and others did work as well. We were watching over them.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that. And We were of them a guardian.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We subdued the devils who would dive into the sea for him and perform other tasks for Solomon. We kept them in his service.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And of the rebellious people there were those who dived for him and did other work besides that, and We kept guard over them;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We also subjected to him some of the jinn who dived for him in the sea and performed other tasks; We kept a watch over them.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And of the evil ones, were some who dived for him, and did other work besides; and it was We Who guarded them.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They made for him whatever he wished, synagogues and statues, dishes large as water-troughs, and cauldrons firmly fixed (on ovens; and We said): "O House of David, act, and give thanks." But few among My creatures are thankful.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They made for him whatever he wished – synagogues and statues, basins like ponds, and large pots built into the ground; “Be thankful, O the people of Dawud!” And few among My bondmen are grateful.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: fashioning for him whatsoever he would -- places of worship, statues, porringers like water-troughs, and anchored cooking-pots. 'Labour, O House of David, in thankfulness; for few indeed are those that are thankful among My servants.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They worked for him whatsoever he pleased, of lofty halls and statues and basins like cisterns and cauldrons Standing firm. Work ye, house of Da'ud! with thanksgiving; few of My bondmen are thankful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They worked for him what he desired, (making) high rooms, images, basins as large as reservoirs, and (cooking) cauldrons fixed (in their places). "Work you, O family of Dawud (David), with thanks!" But few of My slaves are grateful.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They made for him whatever he wished: sanctuaries, statues, bowls like pools, and heavy cauldrons. “O House of David, work with appreciation,” but a few of My servants are appreciative.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They made for him whatever he would desire: stately buildings, images, basins like water-troughs and huge, built-in-cauldrons: “Work, O house of David, in thankfulness (to your Lord). Few of My servants are truly thankful.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They worked for him as he desired on Maharib, Tamathil, large basins like Jawab and Qudur Rasiyat. "Work you, O family of Dawud, with thanks!" But few of My servants are grateful.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They made for him what he willed: synagogues and statues, basins like wells and boilers built into the ground. Give thanks, O House of David! Few of My bondmen are thankful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They made for him whatever he wanted, arches, statues, bowls as basins, and fixed cauldrons. (We said:) 'Give thanks, House of David and work' Yet only a few of My worshipers are thankful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They made for him what he willed of elevated chambers, statues, bowls like reservoirs, and stationary kettles. [We said], "Work, O family of David, in gratitude." And few of My servants are grateful.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They would make for him anything that he wanted like fortresses, statues, large basins like reservoirs, and huge immovable cooking pots. It was said, "Family of David, worship and act gratefully. Only few of my servants are grateful."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They made for him what he pleased of fortresses and images, and bowls (large) as watering-troughs and cooking-pots that will not move from their place; give thanks, O family of Dawood! and very few of My servants are grateful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They made for him whatever he desired: palaces and statues, basins like reservoirs, and large cooking vessels fixed in their places. We said, "Give thanks, house of David, for few of My servants are truly grateful."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They worked for him as he desired, (making) arches, images, basons as large as reservoirs, and (cooking) cauldrons fixed (in their places): "Work ye, sons of David, with thanks! but few of My servants are grateful!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And many others bound in bond.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And other demons bound in chains.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and others also, coupled in fetters:

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And others bound in fetters.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And also others bound in fetters.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And others fettered in chains.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and others that were bound with chains.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And also others bound in fetters.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And others linked together in chains,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and others joined together by (iron) fetters.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And others bound together in shackles.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The rest of the devils were bound in chains.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And others fettered in chains.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and others chained in fetters.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: As also others bound together in fetters.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "This is Our gift," (We said to him), "so bestow freely or withhold without reckoning."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “This is Our bestowal – you may therefore bestow favours or withhold them – you will not be questioned.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'This is Our gift; bestow or withhold without reckoning.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: This is Our gift, SO bestow thou or withhold, without rendering an account.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Saying of Allah to Sulaiman (Solomon)]: "This is Our gift, so spend you or withhold, no account will be asked."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “This is Our gift; so give generously, or withhold; without account.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: “This is Our bestowal. So give or withhold as you wish without account.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: [Allah said to Sulayman]: "This is Our gift, so spend you or withhold, no account will be asked of you."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Saying): This is Our gift, so bestow thou, or withhold, without reckoning.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'This is Our gift, give or withhold without reckoning'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [We said], "This is Our gift, so grant or withhold without account."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We told him, "This is Our gift to you so give them away free or keep them as you like".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: This is Our free gift, therefore give freely or withhold, without reckoning.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We said: "This is Our gift, so give or withhold as you wish without reckoning."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Such are Our Bounties: whether thou bestow them (on others) or withhold them, no account will be asked."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: His armies of jinns and men and Tair assembled, formed into ranks, (and marched)

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And assembled together for Sulaiman were his armies of jinns and men, and of birds – so they had to be restricted.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And his hosts were mustered to Solomon, jinn, men and birds, duly disposed;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And there were gathered unto Sulaiman his hosts of jinns and mankind and birds, and they were set in bands.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And there were gathered before Sulaiman (Solomon) his hosts of jinns and men, and birds, and they all were set in battle order (marching forwards).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: To the service of Solomon were mobilized his troops of sprites, and men, and birds—all held in strict order.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Hosts of jinn and humans and birds were marshalled for Solomon and were kept under full control.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And there were gathered before Sulayman his hosts of Jinn and men, and birds, and they all were set in battle order (marching forward).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And there were gathered together unto Solomon his armies of the jinn and humankind, and of the birds, and they were set in battle order;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gathered to Solomon his army of jinn, humans and birds; gathered and dispersed,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Soloman's army, consisting of human beings, jinn, and birds were gathered together in his presence in ranks.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And his hosts of the jinn and the men and the birds were gathered to him, and they were formed into groups.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Solomon's hosts of jinn and men and birds, were all gathered together in his presence and were ranged in battle order,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And before Solomon were marshalled his hosts,- of Jinns and men and birds, and they were all kept in order and ranks.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Till they reached the Valley of Naml. Said the lady of Naml: "O Naml, go into your dwellings lest Solomon and his hordes should crush you unawares."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Until when they came to the valley of the ants, a she ant exclaimed, “O ants, enter your houses – may not Sulaiman and his armies crush you, unknowingly.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: till, when they came on the Valley of Ants, an ant said, 'Ants, enter your dwelling-places, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you, being unaware!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Until when they came unto the valley of the ants, an ant said: O ants! enter Your habitations lest Sulaiman and his hosts crush you while they perceive not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Till, when they came to the valley of the ants, one of the ants said: "O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulaiman (Solomon) and his hosts crush you, while they perceive not."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Until, when they came upon the Valley of Ants, an ant said, “O ants! Go into your nests, lest Solomon and his troops crush you without noticing.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (Solomon was once on the move with them) until when they reached a valley of ants one of the ants said: "O ants, get into your holes, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you (under their feet) without even knowing."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Till, when they came to the valley of the ants, one of the ants said: "O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulayman and his armies should crush you, while they perceive not."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Till, when they reached the Valley of the Ants, an ant exclaimed: O ants! Enter your dwellings lest Solomon and his armies crush you, unperceiving.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and when they came to the Valley of the Ants, an ant said: 'Ants, go into your dwellings lest Solomon and his army should, unknowingly, crush you'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When they arrived in the valley of the ants, one ant said to the others, "Enter your dwellings lest you be carelessly crushed by Soloman and his army."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Until when they came to the valley of the Naml, a Namlite said: O Naml! enter your houses, (that) Sulaiman and his hosts may not crush you while they do not know.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and when they came to the Valley of the Ants, one ant said, "Ants! Go into your dwellings, in case Solomon and his hosts inadvertently crush you."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: At length, when they came to a (lowly) valley of ants, one of the ants said: "O ye ants, get into your habitations, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you (under foot) without knowing it."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Solomon) smiled, amused at her speech, and said: "O Lord grant me that I should be grateful for the favours You have bestowed on me and my parents, and do good things of Your pleasing; and admit me among Your righteous devotees by Your grace."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He therefore smiled beamingly at her speech*, and submitted, “My Lord, bestow me guidance so that I thank you for the favour which You bestowed upon me and my parents, and so that I may perform the good deeds which please You, and by Your mercy include me among Your bondmen who are worthy of Your proximity.” (Prophet Sulaiman heard the voice of the she ant from far away.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: But he smiled, laughing at its words, and he said, 'My Lord, dispose me that I may be thankful for Thy blessing wherewith Thou hast blessed me and my father and mother, and that I may do righteousness well-pleasing to Thee; and do Thou admit me, by Thy mercy, amongst Thy righteous servants.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So he smiled, amused at her speech, and said: my Lord! arouse me that I should be thankful for Thy favour wherewith Thou hast favoured me and my parents, and that I should work righteously pleasing Thee, and out of Thy mercy enter me among Thine righteous bondmen.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So he [Sulaiman (Solomon)] smiled, amused at her speech and said: "My Lord! Inspire and bestow upon me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your Favours which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds that will please You, and admit me by Your Mercy among Your righteous slaves."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He smiled and laughed at her words, and said, “My Lord, direct me to be thankful for the blessings you have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do good works that please You. And admit me, by Your grace, into the company of Your virtuous servants.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Smiling at the ant's utterance, Solomon burst into laughter and said: "My Lord! Hold me under (Your) control that I may render thanks for the favour which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may act righteously in a manner that would please You. Include me, out of Your Mercy, among Your righteous servants."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So he (Sulayman) smiled, amused at her speech and said: "My Lord! Grant me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your favors which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds that will please You, and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (Solomon) smiled, laughing at her speech, and said: My Lord, arouse me to be thankful for Thy favour wherewith Thou hast favoured me and my parents, and to do good that shall be pleasing unto Thee, and include me in (the number of) Thy righteous slaves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He smiled, and laughed at its words, and said: 'My Lord, inspire me that I should be thankful for Your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do good works that will please You. Admit me, by Your Mercy, among Your righteous worshipers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Solomon) smiled at the ant's remarks and said, "Lord, inspire me to thank you for Your favors to me and my parents and to act righteously so as to please you. Admit me, by your mercy into the company of Your righteous servants".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So he smiled, wondering at her word, and said: My Lord! grant me that I should be grateful for Thy favor which Thou hast bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I should do good such as Thou art pleased with, and make me enter, by Thy mercy, into Thy servants, the good ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Solomon smiled broadly at its words and said, "Lord, inspire me to be thankful for the blessings You have granted me and my parents, and to do good deeds that please You; and include me, by Your grace, among Your righteous servants!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So he smiled, amused at her speech; and he said: "O my Lord! so order me that I may be grateful for Thy favours, which thou hast bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may work the righteousness that will please Thee: And admit me, by Thy Grace, to the ranks of Thy righteous Servants."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When he reviewed the Tair, he said: "How is it I do not see Hud-hud? Is he absent?

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And he surveyed the birds – he therefore said, “What is to me that I do not see the Hudhud (hoopoe), or is he really absent?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And he reviewed the birds; then he said, 'How is it with me, that I do not see the hoopoe? Or is he among the absent?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he sought after the birds and said: what aileth me that I see not the hoopoe; is he among the absentees?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He inspected the birds, and said: "What is the matter that I see not the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then he inspected the birds, and said, “Why do I not see the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (On another occasion) Solomon inspected the birds and said: "Why is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Is he among the absentees?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He inspected the birds, and said: "What is the matter that I see not the hoopoe Or is he among the absentees"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he sought among the birds and said: How is it that I see not the hoopoe, or is he among the absent?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He reviewed the birds and said: 'Why is it that I do not see the hoopoe here? Or is he among the absent?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he took attendance of the birds and said, "Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Solomon) inspected the birds and said, "How is it that I cannot see the hoopoe. Is he absent?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he reviewed the birds, then said: How is it I see not the hoopoe or is it that he is of the absentees?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then Solomon inspected the birds, and said, "How is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Is he absent then?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And he took a muster of the Birds; and he said: "Why is it I see not the Hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I will punish him severely, or cut his throat, unless he bring a valid excuse."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I will indeed punish him severely or slay him, or he must bring to me some clear evidence.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Assuredly I will chastise him with a terrible chastisement, or I will slaughter him, or he bring me a clear authority.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Surely I shall torment him with a severe torment, or I shall slaughter him, unless he bringeth unto me a warranty manifest.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I will surely punish him with a severe torment, or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear reason."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I will punish him most severely, or slay him, unless he gives me a valid excuse.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I will inflict a severe punishment on him or maybe even slaughter him unless he comes forth with a convincing reason (for his absence)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "I will surely punish him with a severe torment or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear reason."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: I verily will punish him with hard punishment or I verily will slay him, or he verily shall bring me a plain excuse.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Surely, I will punish him with a terrible punishment, or I will slaughter him or he gives me a good reason'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I shall certainly punish him severely or slaughter him unless he has a good reason for his absence."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: I will most certainly punish him with a severe punishment, or kill him, or he shall bring to me a clear plea.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I shall surely punish him severely or order him to be executed, unless he gives me a good reason for his absence."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I will certainly punish him with a severe penalty, or execute him, unless he bring me a clear reason (for absence)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But he was not long in coming, and reported: "I have been around where you have not been. I come from Saba with positive news.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So Hudhud did not stay absent for long, and presenting himself submitted, “I have witnessed a matter that your majesty has not seen, and I have brought definite information to you from the city of Saba.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: But he tarried not long, and said, 'I have comprehended that which thou hast not comprehended, and I have come from Sheba to thee with a sure tiding.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Buthe tarried not far, and he said: I have encompassed that which thou hast- not encompassed, and I come unto thee from Saba with a tidings sure.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: But the hoopoe stayed not long, he (came up and) said: "I have grasped (the knowledge of a thing) which you have not grasped and I have come to you from Saba' (Sheba) with true news.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But he did not stay for long. He said, “I have learnt something you did not know. I have come to you from Sheba, with reliable information.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Not before long the hoopoe came up and said: "I have obtained a knowledge which you could not. I have brought for you sure news about Sheba.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But (the hoopoe) stayed not long, he said: "I have grasped which you have not grasped and I have come to you from Saba' with true news."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But he was not long in coming, and he said: I have found out (a thing) that thou apprehendest not, and I come unto thee from Sheba with sure tidings.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He was not long in coming, and said: 'I know what you do not know. I come to you from Sheba with certain news.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But the hoopoe stayed not long and said, "I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Not long after the hoopoe came forward and said, "I have information which you do not have. I have come from the land of Sheba with a true report.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he tarried not long, then said: I comprehend that which you do not comprehend and I have brought to you a sure information from Sheba.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But he was not long in coming, and said, "I have learnt something you did not know. I have come to you from Sheba with reliable news.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But the Hoopoe tarried not far: he (came up and) said: "I have compassed (territory) which thou hast not compassed, and I have come to thee from Saba with tidings true.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I found a woman reigning over them, and she has been favoured with everything; and she has a throne that is magnificent.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I have seen a woman who rules over them, and she has been given from all things, and she has a mighty throne.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given of everything, and she possesses a mighty throne.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily have found a woman ruling over them and she hath been vouchsafed somewhat of everything, and hers is a mighty throne.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given all things that could be possessed by any ruler of the earth, and she has a great throne.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I found a woman ruling over them, and she was given of everything, and she has a magnificent throne.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I found there a woman ruling over them, one who has been endowed with all things and has a mighty throne.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "I found a woman ruling over them, she has been given all things, and she has a great throne."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! I found a woman ruling over them, and she hath been given (abundance) of all things, and hers is a mighty throne.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: There I found a woman ruling over them. She possess everything and has a great throne.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I found a woman ruling the people there and she possessed something of (almost) everything and a great throne.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given abundance and she has a mighty throne:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I found a woman ruling over them, who has been given everything and she has a mighty throne.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I found (there) a woman ruling over them and provided with every requisite; and she has a magnificent throne.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I found that she and her people worship the sun in place of God, for Satan has made their deeds look attractive to them and has turned them away from the Path, so they do not find the way

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I found her and her nation prostrating before the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds seem good to them thereby preventing them from the Straight Path – so they do not attain guidance.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun, apart from God; Satan has decked out fair their deeds to them and he has barred them from the way, and therefore they are not guided,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: I have found her and her people adoring the sun instead of Allah, and the Satan hath made their works fairseeming unto them, and hath barred them from the way, so they are not guided.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah, and Shaitan (Satan) has made their deeds fair-seeming to them, and has barred them from (Allah's) Way, so they have no guidance,"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I found her and her people worshiping the sun, instead of God. Satan made their conduct appear good to them, and diverted them from the path, so they are not guided.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I found that she and her people prostrate themselves before the sun rather than Allah. " Satan has made their deeds appear attractive to them and has, thus, debarred them from the Right Path so they do not find true guidance

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah, and Shaytan has made their deeds fair seeming to them to prevent them from the way, so they have no guidance."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah; and Satan maketh their works fairseeming unto them, and debarreth them from the way (of Truth), so that they go not aright;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But she and her people prostrate to the sun instead of Allah. And satan has made their deeds seem pleasing to them and barred them from the Path, and therefore they are not guided.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I found her and her people prostrating before the sun instead of God. Satan has made their deeds attractive to them. He has kept them away from the right path and they have no guidance.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: I found her and her people adoring the sun instead of Allah, and the Shaitan has made their deeds fair-seeming to them and thus turned them from the way, so they do not go aright

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I found her and her people worshipping the sun, instead of God. Satan has made their conduct appear good to them, and has thus diverted them from the right path, so that they might not be guided.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun besides Allah: Satan has made their deeds seem pleasing in their eyes, and has kept them away from the Path,- so they receive no guidance,-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: To worship God who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and is cognisant of what you hide and what you disclose.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Why do they not prostrate to Allah, Who brings forth the things hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows all what you hide and all what you disclose?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: so that they prostrate not themselves to God, who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and earth; and He knows what you conceal and what you publish.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So that they adore not Allah who bringeth forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knoweth that which conceal and that which ye make known.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Al-La (this word has two interpretations) (A) [As Shaitan (Satan) has barred them from Allah's Way] so that they do not worship (prostrate before) Allah, or (B) So that they may worship (prostrate before) Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 19, Page 149]

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: If only they would worship God, who brings to light the mysteries of the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: that they would prostrate themselves before Allah Who brings to light all that is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows all that you conceal and all that you reveal.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So they do not prostrate themselves before Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So that they worship not Allah, Who bringeth forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knoweth what ye hide and what ye proclaim,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Do they not prostrate themselves to Allah who brings forth all that is concealed in the heavens and earth and He knows what they hide and what they reveal?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allah, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Satan has done this) so that they will not worship God who brings forth whatever is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows whatever you conceal or reveal.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: That they do not make obeisance to Allah, Who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows what you hide and what you make manifest:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Should they not worship God who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and earth and knows both what you conceal and what you make known?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "(Kept them away from the Path), that they should not worship Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what ye hide and what ye reveal.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: God: There is no god but He, the Lord of the glorious throne."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Allah – there is no True God except Him, the Owner of the Great Throne.” (Command of Prostration # 8)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: God: there is no god but He, the Lord of the Mighty Throne.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Allah! there is no God but he, the Lord of the Magnificent Throne.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Allah, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the Lord of the Supreme Throne!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: God—There is no god but He, the Lord of the Sublime Throne.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Allah - none is worthy of worship save He; He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Allah, La ilaha illa Huwa, the Lord of the Supreme Throne!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Allah; there is no Allah save Him, the Lord of the Tremendous Throne.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Allah, there is no god except He, the Lord of the Mighty Throne'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Allah - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: God is the only Lord and master of the Great Throne."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Allah, there is no god but He: He is the Lord of mighty power.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He is God: there is no deity but He, the Lord of the mighty throne."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Allah!- there is no god but He!- Lord of the Throne Supreme!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Solomon) said: 'We shall see if you speak the truth or you are a liar.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Sulaiman, “We shall now see whether you spoke the truth or are among the liars.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said he, 'Now We will see whether thou hast spoken truly, or whether thou art amongst those that lie.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Sulaiman said: we shall see now whether thou hast spoken the truth or whether thou art of the liars.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Sulaiman (Solomon)] said: "We shall see whether you speak the truth or you are (one) of the liars.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “We will see, whether you have spoken the truth, or whether you are a liar.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Solomon said: "Soon shall we see whether you have spoken the truth or are one of those that lie.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: (Sulayman) said: "We shall see whether you speak the truth or you are (one) of the liars."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Solomon) said: We shall see whether thou speakest truth or whether thou art of the liars.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He replied: 'We shall see if what you have said is true or whether you are among those who lie.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Solomon said, "We shall see whether you are truthful or a liar.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: We will see whether you have told the truth or whether you are of the liars:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Solomon said, "We shall soon see whether you have spoken the truth, or whether you are a liar.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
