translation.text: (Solomon) said: "Soon shall we see whether thou hast told the truth or lied!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Take this letter from me, and deliver it to them and withdraw, then see what reply they give in return."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Go with this letter of mine and drop it upon them – then move aside from them and see what they answer in return.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Take this letter of mine, and cast it unto them, then turn back from them and see what they shall return '

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Go thou with this epistle of mine, and cast it down unto them, and turn aside from them, and see how they return.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Go you with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them, then draw back from them, and see what (answer) they return."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Go with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them; then withdraw from them, and see how they respond.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Take this letter of mine, deliver it to them, and then draw back from them, and observe what they do."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Go you with this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then draw back from them and see what they return."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Go with this my letter and throw it down unto them; then turn away and see what (answer) they return,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Take my letter, and drop it to them. Then turn aside and see what they shall return'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then return and see what their reply will be."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Take this my letter and hand it over to them, then turn away from them and see what (answer) they return.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Go with this letter of mine and lay it before them, then withdraw from them and see how they respond."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Go thou, with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them: then draw back from them, and (wait to) see what answer they return"...

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (The Queen said): "O nobles, a venerable letter has been delivered to me.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: The woman said, “O chieftains, indeed a noble letter has been dropped upon me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: She said, 'O Council, see, a letter honourable has been cast unto me.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: She said: O chiefs! verily there hath been cast unto me an honourable epistle.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: She said: "O chiefs! Verily! Here is delivered to me a noble letter,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: She said, “O Counselors, a gracious letter was delivered to me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The Queen said:" Know my nobles that a gracious letter has been delivered to me.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: She said: "O chiefs! Verily, here is delivered to me a noble letter, "

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (The Queen of Sheba) said (when she received the letter): O chieftains! Lo! there hath been thrown unto me a noble letter.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: She (the Queen of Sheba) said: 'O Council, look, an honorable letter has been dropped to me.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She said, "O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (The Queen of Sheba) said to her officials, "A gracious letter has been dropped before me.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: She said: O chief! surely an honorable letter has been delivered to me

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The Queen of Sheba said, "O Counsellors, an honourable letter has been delivered to me.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (The queen) said: "Ye chiefs! here is delivered to me - a letter worthy of respect.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: It is from Solomon, and (says): 'In the name of Allah, Ar-Rahman, Ar-Rahim.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Indeed it is from Sulaiman, and it is (begins) with ‘Allah – beginning with the name of – the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.’”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: It is from Solomon, and it is "In the Name of God, the Merciful, the Compassionate.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily it is from Sulaiman, and verily it is: in the name of Allah, the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Verily! It is from Sulaiman (Solomon), and verily! It (reads): In the Name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful;

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: It is from Solomon, and it is, ‘In the Name of God, the Gracious, the Merciful.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: It is from Solomon, and it is: "In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Verily, it is from Sulayman, and it (reads): `In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful;'

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! it is from Solomon, and lo! it is: In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: It is from Solomon and it is "In the Name of Allah, the Merciful, the Most Merciful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it reads: 'In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: It reads, 'From Soloman. In the Name of God, the Beneficent and the Merciful.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely it is from Sulaiman, and surely it is in the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: It is from Solomon. It reads, "In the name of God, Most Gracious, Most Merciful,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "It is from Solomon, and is (as follows): 'In the name of Allah, Most Gracious, Most Merciful:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Do not rise against me, but come to me in submission.'

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “That ‘Do not wish eminence above me, and present yourselves humbly to me, with submission.’”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Rise not up against me, but come to me in surrender." '

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Saying: exalt not yourselves against me, and come unto me submissive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Be you not exalted against me, but come to me as Muslims (true believers who submit to Allah with full submission)' "

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Do not defy me, and come to me submissively.’”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (It says): "Do not act towards me with defiance, but come to me in submission."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: `Be you not exalted against me, but come to me submitting (as Muslims).' "

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Exalt not yourselves against me, but come unto me as those who surrender.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Do not rise up against me, but come to me in surrender (Muslims)."

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].' "

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Do not consider yourselves superior to me but come to me as Muslims (in submission)".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Saying: exalt not yourselves against me and come to me in submission.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: do not exalt yourselves above me, but come to me in all submission."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'Be ye not arrogant against me, but come to me in submission (to the true Religion).'"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: ." Advise me, O nobles," she said "in this matter. I do not decide any thing until you concur."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: She said, “O chieftains, advise me in this matter of mine; I do not give a final decision until you are present with me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: She said, 'O Council, pronounce to me concerning my affair; I am not used to decide an affair until you bear me witness.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: She said: O chiefs! counsel me in my affair. I am wont not to resolve on any affair until ye are present with me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: She said: "O chiefs! Advise me in (this) case of mine. I decide no case till you are present with me."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: She said, “O counselors, advise me in this matter of mine. I never make a decision unless you are present.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (After reading the letter) the Queen said: "Nobles, let me have your counsel in this matter for I make no firm decision without you."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: She said: "O chiefs! Advise me in case of mine. I decide no case till you are present with me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: She said: O chieftains! Pronounce for me in my case. I decide no case till ye are present with me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: She said: 'O Council, let me hear your counsel, concerning my affairs, for I am not used to deciding an affair until you bear me witness'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She said, "O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: She said, "My officials, what are your views on this matter? I will not decide until I have your views.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: She said: O chiefs! give me advice respecting my affair: I never decide an affair until you are in my presence.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Now advise me in this, Counsellors. I never decide any affair till I have conferred with you."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She said: "Ye chiefs! advise me in (this) my affair: no affair have I decided except in your presence."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "We are men of valour, and brave fighters. It is for you to decide. So consider what you should command."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “We possess great strength and are great warriors, and the decision is yours, therefore consider what you will command.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'We possess force and we possess great might. The affair rests with thee; so consider what thou wilt command.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: we are owners of power and owners of great violence, but the command is with thee; see then whatsoever thou shalt command.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "We have great strength, and great ability for war, but it is for you to command; so think over what you will command."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “We are a people of might and great courage, but the decision is yours, so consider what you wish to command.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "We are strong and are given to vehement fighting. But the decision is yours. Therefore, consider what you would like to command."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "We have great strength, and great ability for war, but it is for you to command; so think over what you will command."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: We are lords of might and lords of great prowess, but it is for thee to command; so consider what thou wilt command.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They replied: 'We are possessors of force and great might. It is for you to command, so consider what you will'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They replied, "We have great power and valor. You are the commander, so decide as you like".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: We are possessors of strength and possessors of mighty prowess, and the command is yours, therefore see what you will command.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "We are strong and our prowess in battle is great, but the decision is in your hands, so consider what you will command."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "We are endued with strength, and given to vehement war: but the command is with thee; so consider what thou wilt command."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: She said: "Surely when kings enter a city they destroy it and despoil the honour of its nobility. So will they do (to us).

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: She said, “Indeed the kings, when they enter a township, destroy it and disgrace its honourable people; and this is what they do.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: She said, 'Kings, when they enter a city, disorder it and make the mighty ones of its inhabitants abased. Even so they too will do.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: She said: verily the kings, when they enter a City, despoil it, and make the most powerful inhabitants thereof the most abased; so will they do.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: She said: "Verily! Kings, when they enter a town (country), they despoil it, and make the most honourable amongst its people low. And thus they do.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: She said, “When kings enter a city, they devastate it, and subjugate its dignified people. Thus they always do.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The Queen said: "When the kings enter a country they cause corruption in it and abase those of its people who are held in honour. This is what they are wont to do.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: She said: "Verily, kings, when they enter a town, they destroy it and make the most honorable among its people the lowest. And thus they do."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: She said: Lo! kings, when they enter a township, ruin it and make the honour of its people shame. Thus will they do.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: She said: 'When kings enter a village, they ruin it and humiliate its nobles. And this they will do.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She said, "Indeed kings - when they enter a city, they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled. And thus do they do.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: She said, "When Kings enter a town they destroy it and disrespect its honorable people. That is what they will do, too.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: She said: Surely the kings, when they enter a town, ruin it and make the noblest of its people to be low, and thus they (always) do;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: She said, "Surely, when mighty kings invade a country, they despoil it and humiliate its noblest inhabitants, these men will do the same,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She said: "Kings, when they enter a country, despoil it, and make the noblest of its people its meanest thus do they behave.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But I will send them a gift and see what the envoys bring back (in return)."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And I shall send a present to them, then see what reply the envoys bring.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Now I will send them a present, and see what the envoys bring back.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So verily I am going to send a present unto them, and see with what answer the envoys come back.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "But verily! I am going to send him a present, and see with what (answer) the messengers return."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I am sending them a gift, and will see what the envoys bring back.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I will send them a gift and then see with what answer my envoys return."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "But verily, I am going to send him a present, and see with what (answer) the messengers return."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But lo! I am going to send a present unto them, and to see with what (answer) the messengers return.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But I shall send them a gift and see what the messengers bring back'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But indeed, I will send to them a gift and see with what [reply] the messengers will return."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I will send a gift and we shall see what response the Messengers will bring."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And surely I am going to send a present to them, and shall wait to see what (answer) do the apostles bring back.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: but I shall send them a present and see with what reply my envoys will return."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But I am going to send him a present, and (wait) to see with what (answer) return (my) ambassadors."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So, when the envoys came to Solomon he said: "Do you wish to increase my wealth? Yet what God has given me is better than what He has given you. No. Be gratified in your present.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So when the envoy came to Sulaiman, he said, “Are you helping me with wealth? What Allah has bestowed upon me is better than what He has given you; rather it is you who are delighted at your gift.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: But when he came to Solomon he said, 'What, would you succour me with wealth, and what God gave me is better than what He has given you? Nay, but instead you rejoice in your gift!

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when he came unto Sulaiman, he said: are ye going to add riches to me. Then that which Allah hath vouchsafed unto me is better than that which He hath vouchsafed unto you. Aye! it is ye who exult in your present.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So when (the messengers with the present) came to Sulaiman (Solomon), he said: "Will you help me in wealth? What Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay, you rejoice in your gift!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When he came to Solomon, he said, “Are you supplying me with money? What God has given me is better than what He has given you. It is you who delight in your gift.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Now, when (the envoy of the Queen) came to Solomon, he said: "Do you want to aid me with wealth? Whatever Allah has granted me is much more than what He has given you. (Keep for yourselves) your gift in which you are exulting.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, when (the messengers with the gift) came to Sulayman, he said: "Will you help me in wealth What Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay, you rejoice in your gift!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So when (the envoy) came unto Solomon, (the King) said: What! Would ye help me with wealth? But that which Allah hath given me is better than that which He hath given you. Nay it is ye (and not I) who exult in your gift.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But when he came to Solomon, he said: 'Is it wealth that you would give me, when Allah has given me that which is better than He has given to you? No, but instead you rejoice in your gift!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when they came to Solomon, he said, "Do you provide me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, it is you who rejoice in your gift.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When her Messengers came to Soloman, he said, "Have you brought me wealth? What God has given to me is far better than what He has given to you, but you are happy with your gifts.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when he came to Sulaiman, he said: What! will you help me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Nay, you are exultant because of your present;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So when [the envoy] came to Solomon he said, "What! Are you offering me wealth? But that which God has given me is better than that which He has given you. Yet you rejoice in your gift.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Now when (the embassy) came to Solomon, he said: "Will ye give me abundance in wealth? But that which Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay it is ye who rejoice in your gift!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Go back to them. We shall soon come with our armies which they will not be able to face. We shall drive them out of (the land) with ignominy, and they will be humbled."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Go back to them – so we shall indeed come upon them with an army they cannot fight, and degrading them shall certainly drive them out from that city, so they will be humiliated.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Return thou to them; we shall assuredly come against them with hosts they have not power to resist, and we shall expel them from there, abased and utterly humbled.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Go back unto them. Then surely we shall come unto them with hosts which they cannot withstand, and we shall drive them forth therefrom abased and they shall be humbled.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Then Sulaiman (Solomon) said to the chief of her messengers who brought the present]: "Go back to them. We verily shall come to them with hosts that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from there in disgrace, and they will be abased."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Go back to them. We will come upon them with troops they cannot resist; and we will expel them from there, disgraced and humiliated.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Envoy, go back to those who sent you and we shall certainly come upon them with hosts whom they will be unable to resist. We shall drive them out from there, and they will suffer humiliation and disgrace."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Go back to them. We verily, shall come to them with armies that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from there in disgrace, and they will be abased."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Return unto them. We verily shall come unto them with hosts that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from thence with shame, and they will be abased.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Go back then, we shall surely come against them with soldiers of which they have no power to oppose, and we shall drive them from there abased and humiliated'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Return to them, for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter, and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation, and they will be debased."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Go back to your people and we shall soon come there with an army which they will not be able to face. We shall drive them from their town, humble, and disgraced."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Go back to them, so we will most certainly come to them with hosts which they shall have no power to oppose, and we will most certainly expel them therefrom in abasement, and they shall be in a state of ignominy.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Go back to them: we shall most certainly come upon them with forces which they will never be able to withstand, and shall most certainly cause them to be driven from their lands, disgraced and humbled!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Go back to them, and be sure we shall come to them with such hosts as they will never be able to meet: We shall expel them from there in disgrace, and they will feel humbled (indeed)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He (then) said (to his courtiers): "O you nobles, is there any one who can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Sulaiman, “O court members, which one of you can bring me her throne before they come humbled in my presence?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'O Council, which one of you will bring me her throne, before they come to me in surrender?'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: O chiefs! which of you will bring unto me her throne, ere they come unto me submitting themselves?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "O chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me surrendering themselves in obedience?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O notables, which one of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Solomon said: "My nobles, which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me surrendering themselves in obedience (as Muslims)"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O chiefs! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come unto me, surrendering?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And he said: 'O Council, which of you will bring me her throne, before they come to me, Muslims (submissive to Allah)'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Solomon] said, "O assembly [of jinn], which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Solomon asked his people, "Who among you can bring her throne before (she, the queen of Sheba) comes to me submissively?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O chiefs! which of you can bring to me her throne before they come to me in submission?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Solomon then said, "O Counsellors, which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said (to his own men): "Ye chiefs! which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: A crafty jinn said: "I will bring it before you rise from your seat, for I am strong and trustworthy."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: An extremely evil jinn said, “I will bring it in your presence before you disperse the assembly; and I am indeed strong and trustworthy upon it.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: An efreet of the jinns said, 'I will bring it to thee, before thou risest from thy place; I have strength for it

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: A giant from the jinns said: I shall bring it unto thee ere thou arisest from thy place; verily I am strong for it and trusty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: An Ifrit (strong) from the jinns said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place (council). And verily, I am indeed strong, and trustworthy for such work."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: An imp of the sprites said, “I will bring it to you before you rise from your seat. I am strong and reliable enough to do it.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: A stalwart of the jinn said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your council. Surely I have the power to do so and I am trustworthy."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: An `Ifrit from the Jinn said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. And verily, I am indeed strong and trustworthy for such work."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: A stalwart of the jinn said: I will bring it thee before thou canst rise from thy place. Lo! I verily am strong and trusty for such work.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: An efreet (an extremely strong jinn) among the jinn replied: 'I will bring it to you before you rise from your place; I have the strength and am trustworthy'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: A powerful one from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: A monstrous jinn said, "I can bring it before you even stand up. I am powerful and trustworthy".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: One audacious among the jinn said: I will bring it to you before you rise up from your place; and most surely I am strong (and) trusty for it.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: A demon from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you get up from your seat. I am strong and trustworthy enough to do it."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Said an 'Ifrit, of the Jinns: "I will bring it to thee before thou rise from thy council: indeed I have full strength for the purpose, and may be trusted."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But one who had knowledge of the letter, said: "I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye." When Solomon saw it before him, (he said): "This is by the grace of my Lord that He may test me whether I am grateful or I am thankless. Yet if one is grateful, he is grateful for himself, and if one is thankless, then surely my Lord is unconcerned and magnanimous."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said one who had knowledge of the Book, “I will bring it in your majesty’s presence before you bat your eyelid”; then when he saw it set in his presence*, Sulaiman said, “This is of the favours of my Lord; so that He may test me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful; and whoever gives thanks only gives thanks for his own good; and whoever is ungrateful – then indeed my Lord is the Independent (Not Needing Anything), the Owner Of All Praise.” (A miracle which occurred through one of Allah’s friends.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and I am trusty.' Said he who possessed knowledge of the Book, 'I will bring it to thee, before ever thy glance returns to thee.' Then, when he saw it settled before him, he said, 'This is of my Lord's bounty that He may try me, whether I am thankful or ungrateful. Whosoever gives thanks gives thanks only for his own soul's good, and whosoever is ungrateful -- my Lord is surely All-sufficient, All-generous.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: The one who had some knowledge of the Book said: I shall bring it unto thee ere thy eye twinkleth. Then when he saw it placed before him, he said: this is of the grace of my Lord that he may prove me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful. Whosoever giveth thanks he only giveth thanks for his own soul; and whosoever is ungrateful then verily my Lord is Self-sufficient, Munificent.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture said: "I will bring it to you within the twinkling of an eye!" then when [Sulaiman (Solomon)] saw it placed before him, he said: "This is by the Grace of my Lord to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! And whoever is grateful, truly, his gratitude is for (the good of) his ownself, and whoever is ungrateful, (he is ungrateful only for the loss of his ownself). Certainly! My Lord is Rich (Free of all wants), Bountiful."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He who had knowledge from the Book said, “I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you.” And when he saw it settled before him, he said, “This is from the grace of my Lord, to test me, whether I am grateful or ungrateful. He who is grateful, his gratitude is to his own credit; but he who is ungrateful—my Lord is Independent and Generous.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And he who had some knowledge of the Book said: "I will bring it before the twinkling of your eye." When Solomon saw the throne placed firmly beside him, he cried out: "This is by the grace of my Lord so that He may test me whether I give thanks for (His Bounty) or act with ingratitude. Whoever is grateful is so to his own good; and whoever is ungrateful, let him know that my Lord is Immensely Resourceful, Most Bountiful."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture, said: "I will bring it to you within the twinkling of an eye!" Then when he saw it placed before him, he said: "This is by the grace of my Lord -- to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! And whoever is grateful, truly, his gratitude is for himself; and whoever is ungrateful, certainly my Lord is Rich, Bountiful."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture said: I will bring it thee before thy gaze returneth unto thee. And when he saw it set in his presence, (Solomon) said: This is of the bounty of my Lord, that He may try me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful. Whosoever giveth thanks he only giveth thanks for (the good of) his own soul; and whosoever is ungrateful (is ungrateful only to his own soul's hurt). For lo! my Lord is Absolute in independence, Bountiful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But he who had knowledge of the Book, said: 'I will bring it to you before your glance comes back to you' And when he saw it set before him, he (Solomon) said: 'This is a favor from my Lord that He might test me whether I am thankful or ungrateful. Whosoever gives thanks gives thanks only for his (own soul's) good, but he who is ungrateful, truly my Lord is Rich and Generous'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Said one who had knowledge from the Scripture, "I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you." And when [Solomon] saw it placed before him, he said, "This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful - his gratitude is only for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever is ungrateful - then indeed, my Lord is Free of need and Generous."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The one who had knowledge from the Book said, "I can bring it to you before you even blink your eye." When Solomon saw the throne placed before him, he said, "This is a favor from my Lord by which He wants to test whether I am grateful or ungrateful. Whoever thanks God does so for his own good. Whoever is ungrateful to God should know that my Lord is Self-Sufficient and Benevolent."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: One who had the knowledge of the Book said: I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye. Then when he saw it settled beside him, he said: This is of the grace of my Lord that He may try me whether I am grateful or ungrateful; and whoever is grateful, he is grateful only for his own soul, and whoever is ungrateful, then surely my Lord is Self-sufficient, Honored.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But one of them who had some knowledge of the Book said, "I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye." When Solomon saw it placed before him, he exclaimed, "This is by the grace of my Lord, to test whether I am grateful or ungrateful. Whosoever is grateful, it is for the good of his own self; and whosoever is ungrateful, then surely my Lord is self-sufficient and generous."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Said one who had knowledge of the Book: "I will bring it to thee within the twinkling of an eye!" Then when (Solomon) saw it placed firmly before him, he said: "This is by the Grace of my Lord!- to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! and if any is grateful, truly his gratitude is (a gain) for his own soul; but if any is ungrateful, truly my Lord is Free of all Needs, Supreme in Honour!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Turning to his nobles) he said: "Change the appearance of her throne. Let us see if she is rightly guided, or is not guided at all."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Sulaiman, “Disguise her throne in front of her so that we may see whether she finds the way* or becomes of those who remain unknowing.” (*Recognises her throne.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'Disguise her throne for her, and we shall behold whether she is guided or if she is of those that are not guided.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: disguise for her her throne, that we may see whether she be guided or be of those who are not guided.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will be guided (to recognise her throne), or she will be one of those not guided."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “Disguise her throne for her, and we shall see whether she will be guided, or remains one of the misguided.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Solomon said: " Set the throne before her casually, and let us see whether she gets to the Truth or is one of those who are not guided to what is right."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will be guided, or she will be one of those not guided."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will go aright or be of those not rightly guided.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (Then) he said: 'Let her throne be disguised, so that we can see whether she is guided or if she is among those who are not guided'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Then he said, "Make a few changes to her throne and let us see whether she will recognize it or not."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: Alter her throne for her, we will see whether she follows the right way or is of those who do not go aright.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "Disguise her throne. We shall see whether or not she will recognize it."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Transform her throne out of all recognition by her: let us see whether she is guided (to the truth) or is one of those who receive no guidance."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When she arrived, they asked her: "Is your throne like this?" She said: "As though this is it. We had come to have knowledge and already submitted."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Then when she came, it was said to her, “Is your throne like this? She said, “As if this is it! And we came to know about this incident beforehand and submit (to you).”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So, when she came, it was said, 'Is thy throne like this?' She said, 'It seems the same.' 'And we were given the knowledge before her, and we were in surrender,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when she arrived, it was said: is thy throne like unto? She said: it is as though it were it; and we have been vouchsafed the knowledge before this, and we have been Muslims.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So when she came, it was said (to her): "Is your throne like this?" She said: "(It is) as though it were the very same." And [Sulaiman (Solomon) said]: "Knowledge was bestowed on us before her, and we were submitted to Allah (in Islam as Muslims before her)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When she arrived, it was said, “Is your throne like this?” She said, “As if this is it.” “We were given knowledge before her, and we were submissive.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When the Queen arrived, she was asked: "Is your throne like this one?" She said: "It seems as if it is the same. We had already come to know this and we had submitted ourselves."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So when she came, it was said, "Is your throne like this" She said: "As though it were the very same." And he said, "Knowledge was bestowed on us before her, and we had submitted to Allah (as Muslims)."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So, when she came, it was said (unto her): Is thy throne like this? She said: (It is) as though it were the very one. And (Solomon said): We were given the knowledge before her and we had surrendered (to Allah).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when she came she was asked: 'Is your throne like this' And she replied: 'It looks like it' And we were given the knowledge before her, and wereMuslims.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when she arrived, it was said [to her], "Is your throne like this?" She said, "[It is] as though it was it." [Solomon said], "And we were given knowledge before her, and we have been Muslims [in submission to Allah].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When she came she was asked, "Is your throne like this?" She replied, "It seems that this is it. We had received the knowledge before this and were submissive (to Solomon's power)".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when she came, it was said: Is your throne like this? She said: It is as it were the same, and we were given the knowledge before it, and we were submissive.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When she came to Solomon, she was asked, "Is your throne like this?" She replied, "It looks as though it were the same, and we had been given knowledge [of your power] before this, and we have already submitted."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So when she arrived, she was asked, "Is this thy throne?" She said, "It was just like this; and knowledge was bestowed on us in advance of this, and we have submitted to Allah (in Islam)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: She was (in fact) turned away by what she worshipped other than God, for she came of an unbelieving people.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And the thing she used to worship instead of Allah prevented her; she was indeed from the disbelieving people.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: but that she served, apart from God, barred her, for she was of a people of unbelievers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And that which she was wont to worship instead of Allah hindered her; verily she was of an infidel people.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And that which she used to worship besides Allah has prevented her (from Islam), for she was of a disbelieving people.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But she was prevented by what she worshiped besides God; she belonged to a disbelieving people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: What prevented her (from accepting the True Faith) was her worshipping deities other than Allah, for she belonged to an unbelieving people.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Saddaha that which she used to worship besides Allah has prevented her (from Islam), for she was of a disbelieving people.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (all) that she was wont to worship instead of Allah hindered her, for she came of disbelieving folk.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: That which she worshipped, other than Allah, had prevented her, for she came from an unbelieving nation.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And that which she was worshipping other than Allah had averted her [from submission to Him]. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Her idols prevented her from believing in God and she was an infidel.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And what she worshipped besides Allah prevented her, surely she was of an unbelieving people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And that which she used to worship beside God had stopped her [from believing]; for she came of a disbelieving people.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And he diverted her from the worship of others besides Allah: for she was (sprung) of a people that had no faith.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: She was invited to enter the court. When she saw it, she took it for a sheet of water, and (pulling up her skirts) uncovered her legs. (Solomon) told her: "This is paved with tiles of glass." "O Lord," she said, "I have wronged myself, and I submit to the Lord of all the worlds with Solomon."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: It was said to her, “Enter the hall”; and when she saw it she thought it was a pool and bared her shins*; said Sulaiman, “This is only a smooth hall, affixed with glass”; she said, “My Lord, I have indeed wronged myself, and I now submit myself along with Sulaiman to Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.” (* In order to cross it)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: It was said to her, 'Enter the pavilion.' But when she saw it, she supposed it was a spreading water, and she bared her legs. He said, 'It is a pavilion smoothed of crystal. She said, 'My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I surrender with Solomon to God, the Lord of all Being.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: It was said unto her: enter the palace. Then when she saw it,, he deemed it a pool and bared her shanks. He said: verily it is a palace evenly floored with glass. She said: my Lord! verily I wronged my soul, and now submit myself together with Sulaiman unto Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: It was said to her: "Enter As-Sarh" [(a glass surface with water underneath it) or a palace], but when she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she (tucked up her clothes) uncovering her legs, Sulaiman (Solomon) said: "Verily, it is Sarh [(a glass surface with water underneath it) or a palace] paved smooth with slab of glass." She said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, and I submit (in Islam, together with Sulaiman (Solomon), to Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: It was said to her, “Go inside the palace.” And when she saw it, she thought it was a deep pond, and she bared her legs. He said, “It is a palace paved with glass.” She said, “My Lord, I have done wrong to myself, and I have submitted with Solomon, to God, Lord of the Worlds.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: She was told: "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a pool of water and she bared both her calves (to enter into it). Solomon said: "This is a slippery floor of crystal." Thereupon she cried out: "My Lord, I have been inflicting much wrong on myself. Now I submit myself with Solomon to Allah, the Lord of the whole Universe."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: It was said to her: "Enter As-Sarh," but when she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she (tucked up her clothes) uncovering her legs. Sulayman said: "Verily, it is a Sarh Mumarrad of Qawarir." She said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, and I submit, together with Sulayman to Allah, the Lord of all that exits."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: It was said unto her: Enter the hall. And when she saw it she deemed it a pool and bared her legs. (Solomon) said: Lo! it is a hall, made smooth, of glass. She said: My Lord! Lo! I have wronged myself, and I surrender with Solomon unto Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: It was said to her: 'Enter the pavilion' And when she saw it, she thought it was a pool of water, and bared her legs. But he said: 'It is a pavilion smoothed with crystal' She said: 'My Lord, I have wronged myself, and I become a Muslim (submissive) with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the Worlds'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She was told, "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, "Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the worlds."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: She was told to enter the palace. When she saw it, she thought that it was a pool and raised her clothe up to her legs. Solomon said, "This is a palace constructed with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself and I submit myself with Solomon to the will of God, the Lord of the Universe."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: It was said to her: Enter the palace; but when she saw it she deemed it to be a great expanse of water, and bared her legs. He said: Surely it is a palace made smooth with glass. She said: My Lord! surely I have been unjust to myself, and I submit with Sulaiman to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then she was bidden to enter the palace; but when she saw it, she thought it was a deep pool of water, and bared her legs. But Solomon explained, "It is just a palace paved with glass," and she said, "My Lord, I have wronged myself: now I submit myself along with Solomon, to God, the Lord of the Universe."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She was asked to enter the lofty Palace: but when she saw it, she thought it was a lake of water, and she (tucked up her skirts), uncovering her legs. He said: "This is but a palace paved smooth with slabs of glass." She said: "O my Lord! I have indeed wronged my soul: I do (now) submit (in Islam), with Solomon, to the Lord of the Worlds."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When they brought fleet-footed chargers in the evening to show him,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: When fast footed steeds were presented to him at evening.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When in the evening were presented to him the standing steeds,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Recall what time there were presented Unto him, at eventide, s coursers swift-footed.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: When there were displayed before him, in the afternoon, well trained horses of the highest breed [for Jihad (holy fighting in Allah's Cause)].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When the beautiful horses were paraded before him in the evening.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And when one evening well-trained and running horses of noble breed were brought to him

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: When there were displayed before him, in the afternoon, well trained horses of the highest breed.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When there were shown to him at eventide lightfooted coursers

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: When his dressage steeds were presented to him in the evening,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Mention] when there were exhibited before him in the afternoon the poised [standing] racehorses.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When the noble galloping horses were displayed to him one evening,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: When there were brought to him in the evening (horses) still when standing, swift when running--

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When well-bred horses, which were fleet of foot were paraded before him near the close of day,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Behold, there were brought before him, at eventide coursers of the highest breeding, and swift of foot;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "The love of horses is worthy of desire to me for the remembrance of my Lord;" and when they were out of sight, (he said):

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Therefore Sulaiman said, “I cherish the love of these horses*, out of remembrance of my Lord”; he then ordered them to be raced until they vanished in a curtain out of sight. (To be used in holy war.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: he said, 'Lo, I have loved the love of good things better than the remembrance of my Lord, until the sun was bidden behind the veil.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: verily I have loved the love of earthly good above the remembrance of my Lord until the sun hath disappeared behind the veil.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And he said: "Alas! I did love the good (these horses) instead of remembering my Lord (in my 'Asr prayer)" till the time was over, and (the sun) had hidden in the veil (of night).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “I have preferred the love of niceties to the remembrance of my Lord—until it disappeared behind the veil.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: he said: “Lo! I have come to love this wealth on account of the remembrance of my Lord.” And when the horses disappeared,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "I did love the good instead of remembering my Lord," till the time was over, and (the sun) had hidden in the veil (of night).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he said: Lo! I have preferred the good things (of the world) to the remembrance of my Lord; till they were taken out of sight behind the curtain.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: he said: 'Indeed I have loved the love of good things better than the remembrance of my Lord until the sun has vanished behind a veil.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he said, "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good [things] over the remembrance of my Lord until the sun disappeared into the curtain [of darkness]."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: he said, "My love of horses for the cause of God has made me continue watching them until sunset, thus making me miss my prayer".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then he said: Surely I preferred the good things to the remembrance of my Lord-- until the sun set and time for Asr prayer was over, (he said):

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: he said, "I have put the love of good things above the remembrance of my Lord," until [the sun] disappeared behind its veil and the horses disappeared from sight,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And he said, "Truly do I love the love of good, with a view to the glory of my Lord,"- until (the sun) was hidden in the veil (of night):

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Bring them back to me," and he began to rub and stroke their shanks and necks.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He then ordered, “Bring them back to me”; and he began caressing their shins and necks.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Return them to me!' And he began to stroke their shanks and necks.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Bring them back Unto me; and he set about slashing their legs and necks.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then he said "Bring them (horses) back to me." Then he began to pass his hand over their legs and their necks (till the end of the display).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Bring them back to me.” And he began caressing their legs and necks.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (he ordered): “Bring these horses back to me,” and then he began to gently stroke their shanks and necks.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then he said: "Bring them back to me." Then he began to pass his hand over their legs and their necks.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Then he said): Bring them back to me, and fell to slashing (with his sword their) legs and necks.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Bring them back to me' And he hacked their legs and necks (slaughtering them for Allah).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [He said], "Return them to me," and set about striking [their] legs and necks.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He said, "Bring them back to me." Then he started to rub their legs and necks.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Bring them back to me; so he began to slash (their) legs and necks.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: "Bring them back to me!" -- [he said] and began to stroke their legs and their necks.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Bring them back to me." then began he to pass his hand over (their) legs and their necks.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We surely tried Solomon, and placed another body on his throne. So he turned to God

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We indeed tested Sulaiman, and placed a dead body on his throne – he therefore inclined towards His Lord.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Certainly We tried Solomon, and We cast upon his throne a mere body; then he repented.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We tried Sulaiman, and set upon his throne a mere body. Thereafter he was penitent.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And, indeed We did try Sulaiman (Solomon) and We placed on his throne Jasadan (a devil, so he lost his kingdom for a while) but he did return (to his throne and kingdom by the Grace of Allah and he did return) to Allah with obedience and in repentance.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We tested Solomon, and placed a body on his throne; then he repented.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Surely We put Solomon to the test and cast upon his throne a mere body. Thereupon he penitently turned (to Us).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed, We tried Sulayman and We placed on his throne Jasad (a body), and he returned.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And verily We tried Solomon, and set upon his throne a (mere) body. Then did he repent.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Indeed, We tried Solomon and placed a body (of a child) upon his throne, then he repented.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We certainly tried Solomon and placed on his throne a body; then he returned.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We tested Solomon by (causing death to his son) and leaving his body on Solomon's chair. Then he turned to Us in repentance,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We tried Sulaiman, and We put on his throne a (mere) body, so he turned (to Allah).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We tried Solomon by placing upon his throne a [lifeless] body; and thereupon he turned towards Us].

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We did try Solomon: We placed on his throne a body (without life); but he did turn (to Us in true devotion):

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Saying: "O Lord, forgive me, and give me such a dominion as none will merit after me. You are the great bestower."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He said, “My Lord! Forgive me and bestow upon me a kingdom, which shall not befit anyone after me; indeed only You are the Great Bestower.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'My Lord, forgive me, and give me a kingdom such as may not befall anyone after me; surely Thou art the All-giver.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: my Lord! forgive me, and bestow on me a dominion which no one may obtain beside me: verily Thou! Thou art the Bestower.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "My Lord! Forgive me, and bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me: Verily, You are the Bestower."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “My Lord, forgive me, and grant me a kingdom never to be attained by anyone after me. You are the Giver.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He said: “My Lord, forgive me and bestow upon me a kingdom such as none other after me will deserve. Surely You are the Bounteous Giver.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "My Lord! Forgive me, and bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me. Verily, You are the Bestower."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Forgive me and bestow on me sovereignty such as shall not belong to any after me. Lo! Thou art the Bestower.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'Forgive me my Lord, and give me a kingdom the like of which will not befall any after me, surely, You are the Giver'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me. Indeed, You are the Bestower."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: saying, "Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom that no one after me can have the like. You are All-munificent".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: My Lord! do Thou forgive me and grant me a kingdom which is not fit for (being inherited by) anyone after me;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He prayed, "Lord forgive me! Grant me such power as no one after me will have -- You are the Most Generous Provider."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said, "O my Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom which, (it may be), suits not another after me: for Thou art the Grantor of Bounties (without measure).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they follow what devilish beings used to chant against the authority of Solomon, though Solomon never disbelieved and only the devils denied, who taught sorcery to men, which, they said, had been revealed to the angels of Babylon, Harut and Marut, who, however, never taught it without saying: "We have been sent to deceive you, so do not renounce (your faith)." They learnt what led to discord between husband and wife. Yet they could not harm any one or without the dispensation of God. And they learnt what harmed them and brought no gain. They knew indeed whoever bought this had no place in the world to come, and that surely they had sold themselves for something that was vile. If only they had sense!

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And they followed what the devils used to read during the rule of Sulaiman (Solomon – peace and blessings be upon him); and Sulaiman did not disbelieve, but the devils disbelieved – they teach people magic; and that which was sent down to the two angels, Harut and Marut in Babylon; and the two (angels) never taught a thing to anyone until they used to say, “We are only a trial, therefore do not lose your faith” and they used to learn from them that by which they cause division between man and his wife; and they cannot harm anyone by it except by Allah’s command; and they learn what will harm them, not benefit them; and surely they know that whoever bargains for this will not have a share in the Hereafter; and for what an abject thing they have sold themselves; if only they knew!

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and they follow what the Satans recited over Solomon's kingdom. Solomon disbelieved not, but the Satans disbelieved, teaching the people sorcery, and that which was sent down upon Babylon's two angels, Harut and Marut; they taught not any man, without they said, 'We are but a temptation; do not disbelieve.' From them they learned how they might divide a man and his wife, yet they did not hurt any man thereby, save by the leave of God, and they learned what hurt them, and did not profit them, knowing well that whoso buys it shall have no share in the world to come; evil then was that they sold themselves for, if they had but known.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And they follow that which the Satans recited in the reign of Sulaiman; and Sulaiman blasphemed not, but the Satans blasphemed.; teaching people magic; and they follow that also which was sent down unto the two angels in Babil, Harut, and Marut. Unto none the twain taught it until they had said: We are but a temptation, so blaspheme not; but they learned from the twain that wherewith they might separate man from his wife; and they could harm none thereby save by Allah's will. And they have learnt that which harmeth them, and profiteth them not; and assuredly they know that whosoever purchaseth it, his is no portion in the Hereafter. And surely vile is the price for which they have bartered themselves, if they but knew!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They followed what the Shayatin (devils) gave out (falsely of the magic) in the lifetime of Sulaiman (Solomon). Sulaiman did not disbelieve, but the Shayatin (devils) disbelieved, teaching men magic and such things that came down at Babylon to the two angels, Harut and Marut, but neither of these two (angels) taught anyone (such things) till they had said, "We are only for trial, so disbelieve not (by learning this magic from us)." And from these (angels) people learn that by which they cause separation between man and his wife, but they could not thus harm anyone except by Allah's Leave. And they learn that which harms them and profits them not. And indeed they knew that the buyers of it (magic) would have no share in the Hereafter. And how bad indeed was that for which they sold their ownselves, if they but knew.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And they followed what the devils taught during the reign of Salomon. It was not Solomon who disbelieved, but it was the devils who disbelieved. They taught the people witchcraft and what was revealed in Babylon to the two angels Harut and Marut. They did not teach anybody until they had said, “We are a test, so do not lose faith.” But they learned from them the means to cause separation between man and his wife. But they cannot harm anyone except with God's permission. And they learned what would harm them and not benefit them. Yet they knew that whoever deals in it will have no share in the Hereafter. Miserable is what they sold their souls for, if they only knew.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (Instead of this,) they began to follow that (magic) to which the devils falsely attributed (the greatness of) the kingdom of Solomon. In fact Solomon was never involved in any practice of disbelief, but the satans, who taught magic to the people were themselves guilty of disbelief. They were after that thing which was sent to Harut and Marut, the two angels at Babylon. Whenever these two angels taught black art to anyone, they would always give a clear warning beforehand, saying, "We are merely a trial for you; so you should not commit blasphemy. But in spite of this warning, those people used to learn from the angels the art which caused division between husband and wife. Although it was obvious that they could not do any harm to anyone by means of this magic without Allah's permission, yet they learnt that art which could not be profitable even for them but was actually harmful. Moreover, they knew it full well that anyone, who purchased that art, would have no share in the Hereafter. What a vile commodity it was for which they sold off their souls, if they had but known it!

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They followed what the Shayatin (devils) gave out (falsely of the magic) in the lifetime of Sulayman (Solomon). Sulayman did not disbelieve, but the Shayatin (devils) disbelieved, teaching men magic and such things that came down at Babylon to the two angels, Harut and Marut, but neither of these two (angels) taught anyone (such things) till they had said, "We are for trial, so disbelieve not (by learning this magic from us)." And from these (angels) people learn that by which they cause separation between man and his wife, but they could not thus harm anyone except by Allah's leave. And they learn that which harms them and profits them not. And indeed they knew that the buyers of it (magic) would have no share in the Hereafter. And how bad indeed was that for which they sold their own selves, if they but knew.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And follow that which the devils falsely related against the kingdom of Solomon. Solomon disbelieved not; but the devils disbelieved, teaching mankind magic and that which was revealed to the two angels in Babel, Harut and Marut. Nor did they (the two angels) teach it to anyone till they had said: We are only a temptation, therefore disbelieve not (in the guidance of Allah). And from these two (angles) people learn that by which they cause division between man and wife; but they injure thereby no-one save by Allah's leave. And they learn that which harmeth them and profiteth them not. And surely they do know that he who trafficketh therein will have no (happy) portion in the Hereafter; and surely evil is the price for which they sell their souls, if they but knew.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and follow what the devils recited over the Kingdom of Solomon. Solomon did not disbelieve, it is the devils who disbelieved, teaching people magic and that which was sent down upon the angels Harut and Marut in Babylon. They did not teach anyone, until both had said: 'We have been sent as a trial; do not disbelieve' From them they learned how they might separate a husband and his wife. However, they did not harm anyone with it, except by the permission of Allah. Indeed, they learned what harms them and does not profit them, yet they knew that anyone who buys it would have no share in the Everlasting Life. Evil is that for which they have sold their souls, if they but knew!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And they followed [instead] what the devils had recited during the reign of Solomon. It was not Solomon who disbelieved, but the devils disbelieved, teaching people magic and that which was revealed to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut. But the two angels do not teach anyone unless they say, "We are a trial, so do not disbelieve [by practicing magic]." And [yet] they learn from them that by which they cause separation between a man and his wife. But they do not harm anyone through it except by permission of Allah. And the people learn what harms them and does not benefit them. But the Children of Israel certainly knew that whoever purchased the magic would not have in the Hereafter any share. And wretched is that for which they sold themselves, if they only knew.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They followed the incantations that the devils used against the kingdom of Solomon. Solomon did not hide the truth but the devils did. They taught magic to the people and whatever was revealed to the two angels, Harut and Marut, in Babylon. The two angels did not teach anything to anyone without saying, "Our case is a temptation for the people, so do not hide the truth." People learned something from the two angels that could cause discord between a man and his wife. However, they could harm no one except by the permission of God. In fact, the (people) learned things that would harm them and render them no benefit. They knew very well that one who engaged in witchcraft would have no reward in the life hereafter. Would that they had known that they had sold their souls for that which is vile!

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And they followed what the Shaitans chanted of sorcery in the reign of Sulaiman, and Sulaiman was not an unbeliever, but the Shaitans disbelieved, they taught men sorcery and that was sent down to the two angels at Babel, Harut and Marut, yet these two taught no man until they had said, "Surely we are only a trial, therefore do not be a disbeliever." Even then men learned from these two, magic by which they might cause a separation between a man and his wife; and they cannot hurt with it any one except with Allah's permission, and they learned what harmed them and did not profit them, and certainly they know that he who bought it should have no share of good in the hereafter and evil was the price for which they sold their souls; had they but known this.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They pursued what the devils falsely attributed to the kingdom of Solomon. Solomon was not an unbeliever: it is the devils who are unbelievers. They taught people witchcraft, and that which was sent down to Harut and Marut, the two angels in Babylon. But these two taught no one until they had said, "We are but a trial, therefore, do not disbelieve [in God's guidance]." So men learnt from them that by which they caused discord between man and wife; but they harmed no one thereby, except by the command of God; [on the contrary], these people learned that which would harm them and do them no good, knowing full well that whoever acquired [this knowledge] would have no share in the Hereafter. Evil indeed was the [price] for which they sold their souls, if only they had known it.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They followed what the evil ones gave out (falsely) against the power of Solomon: the blasphemers Were, not Solomon, but the evil ones, teaching men Magic, and such things as came down at babylon to the angels Harut and Marut. But neither of these taught anyone (Such things) without saying: "We are only for trial; so do not blaspheme." They learned from them the means to sow discord between man and wife. But they could not thus harm anyone except by Allah's permission. And they learned what harmed them, not what profited them. And they knew that the buyers of (magic) would have no share in the happiness of the Hereafter. And vile was the price for which they did sell their souls, if they but knew!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When We ordained (Solomon's) death, none but the weevil, that was eating away his staff (on which he rested), pointed out to them that he was dead. When he fell down (dead) the jinns realised that if they had knowledge of the Unknown they would never have suffered demeaning labour.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So when We sent the command of death towards him, no one revealed his death to the jinns except the termite of the earth which ate his staff; and when he came to the ground, the truth about the jinns was exposed – if they had known the hidden, they would not have remained in the disgraceful toil.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when We decreed that he should die, naught indicated to them that he was dead but the Beast of the Earth devouring his staff; and when he fell down, the jinn saw clearly that, had they only known the Unseen, they would not have continued in the humbling chastisement.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when We decreed death for him, naught discovered his death to them' save a moving creature of the earth which gnawed away his staff. Then when he fell, the Jinn clearly perceived that, if they had known the unseen they would not have tarried in the ignominious torment.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then when We decreed death for him [Sulaiman (Solomon)], nothing informed them (jinns) of his death except a little worm of the earth, which kept (slowly) gnawing away at his stick, so when he fell down, the jinns saw clearly that if they had known the unseen, they would not have stayed in the humiliating torment.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, when We decreed death for him, nothing indicated his death to them except an earthworm eating at his staff. Then, when he fell down, it became clear to the sprites that, had they known the unseen, they would not have remained in the demeaning torment.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When We executed Our decree of death on Solomon, nothing indicated to the jinn that he was dead except a worm eating away his staff. So when Solomon fell down, the jinn realised that had they known what lies in the realm beyond perception, they would not have continued to be in this humiliating chastisement.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then when We decreed death for him, nothing informed them (Jinn) of his death except a little worm of the earth which kept (slowly) gnawing away at his stick. So when he fell down, the Jinn saw clearly that if they had known the Unseen, they would not have stayed in the humiliating torment.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when We decreed death for him, nothing showed his death to them save a creeping creature of the earth which gnawed away his staff. And when he fell the jinn saw clearly how, if they had known the Unseen, they would not have continued in despised toil.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when We decreed (Solomon's) death, they had no indication that he was dead until (they saw a termite), a crawler of the earth eating away his staff. And when he fell down, the jinn realized that had they known the unseen, they would not have continued in their humiliating punishment.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And when We decreed for Solomon death, nothing indicated to the jinn his death except a creature of the earth eating his staff. But when he fell, it became clear to the jinn that if they had known the unseen, they would not have remained in humiliating punishment.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When We decreed that Solomon should die, no one knew of his death except for a creeping creature of the earth who ate-up his staff. When he fell down, the jinn realized that if they had known about the unseen, they would not have remained in such a humiliating torment for so long.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But when We decreed death for him, naught showed them his death but a creature of the earth that ate away his staff; and when it fell down, the jinn came to know plainly that if they had known the unseen, they would not have tarried in abasing torment.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When We decreed Solomon's death, nothing indicated his death to them except a worm from the earth, which was eating away at his cane. When he fell down, the jinn plainly realized that if they had known what was unseen, they would not have had to continue with such humiliating labour.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then, when We decreed (Solomon's) death, nothing showed them his death except a little worm of the earth, which kept (slowly) gnawing away at his staff: so when he fell down, the Jinns saw plainly that if they had known the unseen, they would not have tarried in the humiliating Penalty (of their Task).

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Abu Huraira: The Prophet said, "Solomon (the son of) David said, 'Tonight I will sleep with seventy ladies each of whom will conceive a child who will be a knight fighting for "Allah's Cause.' His companion said, 'If Allah will.' But Solomon did not say so; therefore none of those women got pregnant except one who gave birth to a half child." The Prophet further said, "If the Prophet Solomon had said it (i.e. 'If Allah will') he would have begotten children who would have fought in Allah's Cause." Shuaib and Ibn Abi Az-Zinad said, "Ninety (women) is more correct (than seventy)."

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Prophet Solomon who had sixty wives, once said, "Tonight I will have sexual relation (sleep) with all my wives so that each of them will become pregnant and bring forth (a boy who will grow into) a cavalier and will fight in Allah's Cause." So he slept with his wives and none of them (conceived and) delivered (a child) except one who brought a half (body) boy (deformed). Allah's Prophet said, "If Solomon had said; 'If Allah Will,' then each of those women would have delivered a (would-be) cavalier to fight in Allah's Cause." (See Hadith No. 74 A, Vol. 4).
